name: ESSAI 2026
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will be held in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and tracks covering topics such as Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, targeting PhD students and young researchers. The program includes networking opportunities, social events like a reception at Vienna City Hall, and involvement of over 30 international lecturers.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at a mountain‑side hotel near Oslo, offering a scenic setting with skiing and walking opportunities. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and focuses on multilingual large language models, evaluation methods, and data curation for European languages. The program combines technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and visits to the National Library of Norway, and participation is subsidised by the OpenEuroLLM project with no registration fee.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: ProbAI School 2026 is a Probabilistic AI School taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania. The school aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence topics. It welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals for a five‑day program in early August.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of topics in computer vision, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a programme offered by the Oxford ML School that combines in‑person sessions in Oxford with an online component. It provides three specialised tracks covering the latest AI theory and applications, taught by world‑renowned experts from leading academic and industry labs. Participants can customise their learning by selecting any combination of tracks to match their individual goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is hosted at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh that brings together master’s‑level students and industry practitioners to explore advanced robotics topics. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials, and hands‑on sessions covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI, and real‑world applications, with industry presentations and networking opportunities. The school takes place at the National Robotarium and Heriot‑Watt University’s postgraduate centre, providing access to state‑of‑the‑art facilities and a special summer school dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals such as forensic examiners and police officers, offering intensive lectures by leading experts, hands‑on sessions, and optional video‑conference participation.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: The AIces 2026 International School focuses on the cognitive, ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence. It is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium, and open‑debate sessions. The event takes place in Porto, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026, with both face‑to‑face interaction and live online participation, and recordings available for 45 days afterwards.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, multidisciplinary hands‑on training, a poster session, and a social program to foster networking among students, researchers, and professionals. The three‑day event runs from 11 to 13 March 2026 and targets participants interested in the societal implications of AI and autonomous technologies.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) 2026 takes place at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through courses and informal networking. The programme features expert talks on topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and hands‑on sessions on human‑robot interaction, set against the backdrop of the Vercors mountains.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place in Melbourne, Australia at the Sandridge Events Centre in Port Melbourne. It targets 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, offering two weeks of lectures and tutorials covering core theory and emerging topics beyond large language models. World‑class speakers from academia and industry present on subjects such as deep learning, generative modeling, reinforcement learning, and AI in industry, with stipends available for selected participants.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The Winter School, part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference 2027, will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from 11 to 15 January 2027 and focuses on state-of-the-art deep-learning techniques such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time-series prediction and speech recognition. The program brings together national and international researchers to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and present cutting‑edge research in deep learning.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, labs, and project work. It covers topics from fundamental mathematics and statistics to cutting‑edge AI methods and applications, targeting senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty, and industry professionals. The school aims to build AI expertise in Nepal and the region by bringing together international experts and over 500 in‑person participants.

name: EEML Summer School
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers a series of lectures and tutorials by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, and the University of Oxford, covering topics in machine learning and AI. The program is aimed at students and early‑career researchers seeking intensive training and networking opportunities in the field.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program featuring keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, focusing on topics such as computer vision, NLP, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. The school also provides networking opportunities through poster sessions, social activities, and informal interactions with speakers and sponsors.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master’s, Ph.D., post‑doctoral and early‑career researchers, offering lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship on state‑of‑the‑art multimodal and generative AI techniques. Participants also benefit from networking events and interactions with leading AI experts from academia and industry.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 (AthNLP) will be held at the campus of NCSR Demokritos in Athens, Greece. It offers intensive training on NLP topics for students and researchers, organized jointly by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, and Heriot‑Watt University. The program runs from September 2 to 8, 2026 and is followed by the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall offers an intensive program on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning. It combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on projects covering clustering, GANs, self‑supervised learning, and other state‑of‑the‑art techniques across various domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are considering graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI is organized in Caen, Normandy, France and targets researchers, PhD students, and post‑doctoral scholars. It covers machine learning for scientific data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and the integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops and case studies. The program emphasizes deep‑learning techniques, requiring participants to have experience with Python, NumPy, and PyTorch, and aims to equip them with skills to accelerate scientific discovery.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and innovative business‑technology topics. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students and combines classroom teaching, workshops, group work and social activities on the Lappeenranta campus in Finland. The programme includes both on‑site sessions from 3 – 7 August 2026 and optional online components, providing an academic and cultural experience in the Finnish summer.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School, a Machine Learning Crash Course, takes place at the University of Genoa in Genoa, Italy. It offers an introductory, hands‑on program covering fundamental concepts, algorithms and practical lab sessions, aimed at undergraduate and graduate students as well as professionals. The course is held entirely in person at the DIBRIS/DIMA building, includes daily lectures and labs, and provides university credit upon successful completion.
